LHX.sentence: L3Harris ticked up a little today, and while its profit margins are running thinner than the bigger defense companies right now, steady military spending means the underlying business is fine.

ASML.sentence: ASML slipped about 5% today, but that's just a normal breather after a strong run — they're still the only company in the world making a certain essential chipmaking machine.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having a quiet, steady week — they supply industrial gases under long-term contracts and simply keep chugging along, which is the whole story with this one.

MSFT.sentence: No fresh data on Microsoft today, so there's nothing new to report.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher rose today and looks unusually inexpensive compared to its own history, even though the business of selling lab equipment to biotech and hospitals hasn't fundamentally changed.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge dipped slightly, and its pipeline 'toll road' income is still generous, though the payout is about as rich as it's been in years.

NOW.sentence: No fresh data on ServiceNow today, so there's nothing new to report.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore was basically flat today; its profits are in a deep slump right now, which for a mining and trading company often marks the low point of the cycle rather than trouble ahead.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin nudged up today but is still well below where it was earlier in the year, which is the kind of quiet, beaten-down stretch that can set up a recovery.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA was essentially flat, and since most of its earnings come from Mexico, the calm, stable Mexican peso means there's little drama here.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras eased back a touch, but it's still paying out a very generous dividend while oil prices hold up, so it remains a strong income story.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk, the Danish shipping giant that hauls containers across the oceans, dropped about 5% today and is now valued below the worth of its own fleet — a level that has historically marked a bottom in the shipping cycle.
